ground_truth_en: Zarathustra left his home and went to the mountains when he was thirty years old.
summary_ko: 서른 살에 고향과 호수를 떠나 산으로 들어갔다.

ground_truth_en: Zarathustra spent ten years in the mountains, enjoying his spirit and solitude before descending.
summary_ko: 십 년을 산에서 보냈다. 자신의 정신과 고독을 즐겼다.

ground_truth_en: An eagle and a serpent accompany Zarathustra — the eagle representing pride and the serpent representing wisdom.
summary_ko: 독수리와 뱀. 독수리는 긍지를, 뱀은 지혜를 상징.

ground_truth_en: The old man Zarathustra meets in the forest is a saint who has retreated from human society to praise God.
summary_ko: 인간 세상을 떠나 숲에서 신을 찬양하는 늙은 성자.

ground_truth_en: The saint composes hymns, weeps, laughs, and grumbles to praise God in the forest.
summary_ko: 찬가를 짓고, 울고 웃고 중얼거리며 신을 찬양하고 있었다.

ground_truth_en: Zarathustra refrains from telling the saint that 'God is dead', leaving him in his devotional life.
summary_ko: '신은 죽었다'는 사실. 성자가 모른 채 신을 찬양하도록 두고 떠남.

ground_truth_en: The Übermensch (Superman/Overman) is the goal humanity should strive toward — a being who overcomes the current human condition and creates new values after the death of God.
summary_ko: 인간이 극복해야 할 목표. 신의 죽음 이후 새 가치를 창조하는 존재.

ground_truth_en: 'God is dead' means the collapse of metaphysical and religious absolutes in modernity; humanity can no longer ground its values in a transcendent being and must create values itself.
summary_ko: 형이상학적·종교적 절대성의 붕괴. 인간이 스스로 가치를 창조해야 함.

ground_truth_en: Zarathustra descends to teach humanity about the Übermensch — that man is something to be overcome, and to share his accumulated wisdom.
summary_ko: 위버멘쉬의 가르침. 인간은 극복되어야 할 무엇이라는 것.

ground_truth_en: Humanity is a bridge or rope stretched between beast and Übermensch; the current human is something to be surpassed, not an end in itself.
summary_ko: 인간은 짐승과 위버멘쉬 사이에 걸쳐진 줄. 극복의 대상이지 종착점이 아님.

ground_truth_en: Spirit (Geist) in Nietzsche refers to the active, creative force of the self that undergoes transformations — from camel (bearing burdens) to lion (rejecting old values) to child (creating new values).
summary_ko: 자기를 변화시키는 창조적 힘. 낙타 → 사자 → 어린아이의 세 변화.

ground_truth_en: The 'great star' refers to the sun, which Zarathustra addresses at dawn; like the sun whose happiness requires those it illuminates, Zarathustra's wisdom requires recipients — giving and overflowing are essential.
summary_ko: 떠오르는 태양. 비춰줄 자가 없으면 행복도 없듯, 차라도 베풀 자가 필요함.

ground_truth_en: The bee gathering too much honey symbolizes Zarathustra weary of his own accumulated wisdom; he needs hands reaching out to receive what he must give away — the act of bestowing.
summary_ko: 차라가 자신의 지혜에 지쳐 베풀 손이 필요한 상태를 비유.

ground_truth_en: Going-under (Untergang) is Zarathustra's willing descent from the mountain — a self-sacrifice and transition; in Nietzsche it is the precondition for overcoming and creation, the dignified setting-down like the sun at evening.
summary_ko: 산에서 내려감 = 자발적 희생·이행. 극복과 창조의 전제 조건.

ground_truth_en: The eagle symbolizes pride (the proudest animal under the sun, soaring high) and the serpent symbolizes wisdom (the wisest animal, knowing the earth); together they accompany Zarathustra's solitude.
summary_ko: 독수리 = 긍지(높이 나는 자긍심), 뱀 = 지혜(땅을 아는 영민함).

text: 차라투스트라는 왜 '다시 인간이 되어야 한다'고 말하나?
ground_truth_en: After ten years of solitude that made him transcendent, Zarathustra must descend and 'become man again' — return to humanity to share his wisdom, completing the cycle of withdrawal and bestowal.
summary_ko: 10년의 고독으로 초인적이 된 자신을 인간 세상으로 돌이키겠다는 결심.

ground_truth_en: Zarathustra is the German form of Zoroaster, the ancient Persian prophet who founded Zoroastrianism; Nietzsche chose this figure ironically as the one who first introduced moral dualism and thus must undo it.
summary_ko: 고대 페르시아 종교(조로아스터교)의 창시자 조로아스터의 독일어식 표기.

text: 니체는 왜 이 인물을 주인공으로 삼았나?
ground_truth_en: Nietzsche chose Zarathustra because the historical Zoroaster first established the moral dualism of good and evil; the same prophet must now reverse this error, recognizing the mistake from its origin.
summary_ko: 도덕적 이원론을 처음 세운 자가 그것을 거두는 자가 되어야 한다는 아이러니.

ground_truth_en: Thus Spoke Zarathustra was written between 1883 and 1885 in four parts; it is Nietzsche's late-period masterpiece, written in a poetic-philosophical style.
summary_ko: 1883~1885년 사이. 니체 후기의 대표작으로 4부작.

text: 차라투스트라가 시장에서 군중에게 외친 첫 말은?
ground_truth_en: Zarathustra's first proclamation to the crowd is "I teach you the Übermensch. Man is something that shall be overcome."
summary_ko: "나는 그대들에게 위버멘쉬를 가르치노라. 사람은 극복되어야 할 무엇이다."

ground_truth_en: While crossing a rope between two towers, the tightrope walker is overtaken by a jester who leaps over him with a devilish cry, causing him to fall to his death.
summary_ko: 두 탑 사이의 줄을 건너던 중, 뒤따라 나온 어릿광대가 그를 뛰어넘으며 떨어뜨림.

ground_truth_en: The dying tightrope walker fears the devil will drag his soul to hell; Zarathustra reassures him there is no devil, no hell, and his soul will die even before his body.
summary_ko: 자기 영혼이 몸보다 먼저 죽을까 두렵다고 했고, 차라는 그렇지 않다고 위로함.

ground_truth_en: Zarathustra carries the dead tightrope walker's corpse on his back out of the city and lays it in a hollow tree to protect it from wolves.
summary_ko: 시신을 어깨에 메고 도시를 떠나 숲으로 가서 나무 구멍에 묻음.

ground_truth_en: The Last Man is the contemptible figure who seeks only comfort and small pleasures, having lost all aspiration and the capacity for self-overcoming; he is the antithesis of the Übermensch.
summary_ko: 안락과 행복만을 추구하며 동경을 잃은 경멸스러운 존재. 위버멘쉬의 반대 형상.

ground_truth_en: The Übermensch creates new values through self-overcoming, while the Last Man abandons aspiration and contents himself with small pleasures and security; they represent opposite human possibilities after the death of God.
summary_ko: 위버멘쉬는 자기를 극복하여 새 가치를 창조, 마지막 인간은 극복을 포기하고 안락에 머무름.

ground_truth_en: The crowd rejects Zarathustra's teaching of the Übermensch and instead demands he give them the Last Man — they prefer comfortable mediocrity over the difficult task of self-overcoming.
summary_ko: 군중은 위버멘쉬보다 마지막 인간을 원함. 안락한 행복을 달라고 외침.

text: '사람은 짐승과 위버멘쉬 사이에 걸쳐진 줄'이라는 뜻은?
ground_truth_en: Humanity is a rope stretched between beast and Übermensch — a transition, not a destination; what is valuable in man is that he is a bridge, the act of crossing over and going under itself.
summary_ko: 인간은 종착점이 아니라 다리, 건너가는 행위 자체가 가치 있음.

ground_truth_en: Self-overcoming is the continuous process of surpassing one's current self to create a new self — the essential activity that distinguishes those moving toward the Übermensch from the Last Man who refuses to overcome.
summary_ko: 기존 자기를 넘어 새로운 자기를 창조하는 끊임없는 과정. 위버멘쉬로 가는 길.

ground_truth_en: The tightrope walker symbolizes humanity itself — a being who attempts the dangerous crossing between beast and Übermensch; what is admirable is the act of crossing, even if one falls.
summary_ko: 인간 자체의 상징. 짐승과 위버멘쉬 사이를 건너는 자, 건너가는 행위 그 자체.

ground_truth_en: The falling walker's death symbolizes that even failed overcoming is honorable; Zarathustra treats him as a friend and gives him proper burial because he 'made danger his vocation' — the attempt itself has dignity.
summary_ko: 건너가다 실패해도 그 시도 자체가 가치 있음. 차라가 그를 친구로 대함.

ground_truth_en: The marketplace symbolizes the realm of the crowd and the buffoons — a space of noise, flies, and mediocrity where genuine teaching cannot take root; Zarathustra learns he should not address the herd.
summary_ko: 군중과 광대들의 공간. 진정한 가르침이 통하지 않는 천박함의 상징.

ground_truth_en: The 'overgoers' (Übergehende) cross toward the Übermensch and the 'down-goers' (Untergehende) sacrifice themselves; both are loved by Zarathustra because going-under is necessary for going-over — destruction precedes creation.
summary_ko: 위버멘쉬로 건너가는 자, 자신을 희생해 몰락하는 자. 둘 다 사랑받을 만한 자.

ground_truth_en: The rope over the abyss represents the dangerous position of human existence — suspended between beast and Übermensch, where every step risks falling; the danger itself is what makes humanity worth loving.
summary_ko: 인간 존재의 위험한 위치. 건너가기와 떨어지기 모두 가능한 긴장.

ground_truth_en: The crowd in the marketplace is gathered to watch a tightrope walker performance — ordinary townspeople interested in spectacle and entertainment, representing the 'herd' who rejects Zarathustra's teaching.
summary_ko: 줄타기 광대 공연을 보러 모인 도시 군중. 새로운 사상엔 관심 없는 평범한 자들.

ground_truth_en: Zarathustra's first teaching fails because he addressed the wrong audience — the marketplace crowd wants comfort, not self-overcoming; he learns he must seek companions, not preach to the herd.
summary_ko: 군중은 위버멘쉬가 아닌 마지막 인간을 원함. 차라는 잘못된 청자에게 말했음.
